After the people of Israel had crossed the dry riverbed, the Lord commanded Joshua to select twelve men, one from each tribe, to carry on their shoulders twelve stones from the Jordan and lay them down in the place where they would encamp that night. Then he added, "That this may be a sign among you, that when your children ask their fathers in time to come, saying, What mean ye by these stones?…

That We May Be One By Elder Howard W. Hunter Of the Council of the Twelve At the time of the conquest of western Palestine after the death of Moses, the ten tribes of ancient Israel were united under Joshua. Preparations had been made and orders given for the camp to make ready to cross the Jordan and lay siege to Jericho. Joshua told the people that the Lord would do wonders by drying up the…

When we are dealing with the need each of us has in times of stress to walk to the edge of the light before asking the Lord to spell everything out for us, why not take the episode of the Red Sea, which is more dramatic and on a larger scale, and link it with the "smaller" episode in the third chapter of Joshua when the children of Israel had to cross the flood-swollen Jordan River and the Lord…

dried up the waters of Jordan from before the children of Israel, until we were passed over, that their heart melted, neither was there spirit in them any more, because of the children of Israel.
